text:
Since the war began, the Gulf monarchies have warned people against sharing videos of Iranian attacks, citing security concerns and, in some cases, threatening prosecution. The Emirates had strict limits on freedom of expression even before the joint U.S.-Israeli assault on Iran started. Its image as a safe haven has been damaged by the retaliatory strikes from Iran, which have hurt businesses and tourism.
Last week, the Emirates’ public prosecutor issued a statement saying that taking or sharing videos documenting airstrikes and damage — or A.I.-generated material claiming to show Iranian attacks — “is illegal if it incites panic among the public, spreads false news, or harms public security and order.”
“The Public Prosecution will take legal action against perpetrators without leniency,” the statement added. “The Attorney General appealed to citizens and residents to abide by the law.”
title: Britain warns its citizens in Dubai they could be jailed for sharing photos of damage from airstrikes.

citability.title: Britain warns its citizens in Dubai they could be jailed for sharing photos of damage from airstrikes.
